summary: nodemon typosquat pulling unused transitive dependency ts-webplug
details: Package is published as `nodemon-gulp` but its contents are a verbatim copy of the upstream `nodemon` project: `package.json` declares `author: Remy Sharp`, `homepage: https://nodemon.io`, and `bin: { "nodemon": "./bin/nodemon.js" }`, so installing this package places a `nodemon` shim on the user's PATH supplied by an unrelated publisher. `package.json` also declares `"ts-webplug": "3.0.5"` as a runtime dependency, yet no file under lib/ or bin/ ever `require()`s `ts-webplug`. The only effect of declaring this dep is to silently pull `ts-webplug` into the installer's dependency tree on `npm install`, where its lifecycle and import-time code can execute. This is the canonical namespace-abuse + typosquat-loader shape: a clone of a well-known package, republished under a confusable name, used as a vehicle to deliver an otherwise-unjustified third-party dependency to anyone who installs it.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
